item.name: ACA Coverage
Return a Veteran's enrollment status in minimum essential coverage (MEC) as defined by the Affordable Care Act (ACA). (restricted access).

request.name: Return a Veteran's enrollment status in minimum essential coverage (MEC) as defined by the Affordable Care Act (ACA). (restricted access).

description.content:
This endpoint checks a Veteran's enrollment status for minimum essential
  coverage (MEC), as defined by the Affordable Care Act (ACA), for a specified
  date range.

  The response includes a code for Veteran's health benefits status for
  the request's date range. If the status returned is "APPLICANT_ENROLLED,"
  the response also includes the start and end dates of the coverage. If multiple
  enrollments are found for the requested date range, multiple start and end dates are
  returned (limited to the 12 most recent enrollments).

  The ACA Coverage endpoint requires all of the following data elements in your request:
  * First Name
  * Last Name
  * Date of Birth (format: "yyyy-mm-dd")
  * Social Security Number (format: "000-00-0000")
  * ACA Coverage Search Start Date (format: "yyyy-mm-dd")
  * ACA Coverage Search End Date (format: "yyyy-mm-dd")

  The required data are utilized to match a Veteran in VA records.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

item.name: Search for information about a Veteran's VA disability ratings using demographic data. Data returned includes individual and combined ratings, diagnoses, and eligibility dates for compensation for service-connected conditions.

request.name: Search for information about a Veteran's VA disability ratings using demographic data. Data returned includes individual and combined ratings, diagnoses, and eligibility dates for compensation for service-connected conditions.

description.content:
This endpoint provides a Veteran's disability ratings and related decisions and dates.

Required information:
* First Name
* Last Name
* Date of Birth
* Street Address
* City
* State
* Country
* Zipcode

Optional information:
* Middle Name
* Gender
* Mother's Maiden Name
* Home Phone Number
* Birth Place City
* Birth Place State
* Birth Place Country

Tips for finding a match:
* First and Last Name, Address and DOB are often essential to uniquely identifying
a Veteran.
* Home Address is recommended, but it may be beneficial to retry the search with
additional addresses if they are available and the first attempt is not confirmed.
* To find a match, it can help to retry with different demographic information,
such as alternate last names or addresses.
* It can also help to remove special characters, hyphens, apostrophes, or spaces
in a name prior to matching.

NOTE: In the Sandbox only DOB and ZIP are required to identify the test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name: Disability Rating retrieved successfully

item.name: Search for information about a Veteran's disability rating using Social Security number and demographic data Data returned includes individual and combined ratings, diagnoses, and eligibility dates for compensation for service-connected conditions.(restricted access).

request.name: Search for information about a Veteran's disability rating using Social Security number and demographic data Data returned includes individual and combined ratings, diagnoses, and eligibility dates for compensation for service-connected conditions.(restricted access).

description.content:
This endpoint provides a Veteran's combined and individual disability ratings and
related decisions and dates. It allows searching for a Veteran using Social Security number and demographic information.

If using Social Security number, the following information is required:
* Social Security number
* First name
* Last name
* Birth date

If using demographic data, the following information is required:
* First Name
* Last Name
* Birth date
* Street Address
* City
* State
* Country
* Zipcode

The following optional information may also be used with Social Security number
or demographic data:
* Middle Name
* Gender
* Mother's Maiden Name
* Home Phone Number
* Birth Place City
* Birth Place State
* Birth Place Country

Tips for finding a match:
* First and Last Name, Address and Birth date are often essential to uniquely
identifying a Veteran.
* Home address is recommended, but it may also help to retry the search with
additional available addresses when the first attempt is not confirmed.
* To find a match, it can help to retry with different demographic information,
such as alternate last names or addresses.
* It can also help to remove special characters, hyphens, apostrophes, or spaces
in a name prior to matching.

NOTE: In the Sandbox only birth date and zip code are required to identify the
test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name: Disability Rating retrieved successfully

item.name: Enrolled Benefits
Get enrolled benefits information.

description.content:
This endpoint returns select enrolled benefits for a Veteran.
If the Veteran is enrolled, the codes and names for benefits they are
receiving will be returned for the following benefit types:

* Compensation (Code C)
* 306 Pension (Code 306P)
* Old Law Pension (Code OLP)
* Improved Pension (Code IP)

item.name: Flashes
Retrieve certain benefit flashes associated with a Veteran.

request.name: Retrieve certain benefit flashes associated with a Veteran.

description.content:
A flash is an attribute on a Veteran's profile that describes special
circumstances which can apply to a Veteran. Flashes can aid in
properly routing claims or determining eligibility for some VA
benefits.

This endpoint currently shares the following flashes
if they have been assigned to the Veteran.

* Agent Orange (AO) Exposure Verified
* Gulf War Veteran PACT Eligibility Verified

If neither of these flashes exist for the Veteran, then a 200 response
with an empty dataset will be returned.

If you have a use case for additional flashes, please
contact us with the details.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name: Returns list of select flashes for a given Veteran

item.name: Search for information about a Veteran's P&T disability and IU disability statuses and effective dates, based on demographic data.

request.name: Search for information about a Veteran's P&T disability and IU disability statuses and effective dates, based on demographic data.

description.content:
This endpoint returns a Veteran's information about permanent and total (P&T) disability and total disability based on Individual Unemployability (IU) statuses and effective dates.

  Required information:

  * First name
  * Last name
  * Date of birth
  * Street address
  * City
  * State
  * Country
  * ZIP code

  Optional information:

  * Middle name
  * Gender
  * Mother's maiden name
  * Home phone number
  * Birthplace city
  * Birthplace state
  * Birthplace country

  Tips for finding a match:

  * Use first and last name, address, and date of birth  for best results. Using a home address is recommended.
  * Retry with different demographic information, such as  alternate addresses or last names, if no match is found.
  * Try removing special characters and spaces in a name.

  NOTE: In the sandbox environment, only date of birth  and ZIP code are required to identify the test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name: Permanent And Total Disability retrieved successfully

 If using a Social Security number,  the following information is required:

 If using demographic data,  the following information is required:

 Tips for finding a match:

 * Use first and last name, address, and date  of birth for best results. Using a home  address is recommended.
 * Retry with different demographic information, such   as alternate addresses or last names, if no match is found.
 * Try removing special characters and spaces in a name.

 NOTE: In the sandbox environment, only date  of birth and ZIP code are required to identify the test user.

item.name: Service History
Retrieve the service history of a Veteran.

request.name: Retrieve the service history of a Veteran.

description.content: This endpoint provides a Veteran's detailed service history, including all military service episodes with the first and last name of the Veteran, the branch of service, start and end dates, paygrade, discharge status, and separation reason. It also provides start and end dates and locations for each deployment within each episode of service.

item.name: Retrieve the service history of a Veteran using demographics to search for the Veteran.

request.name: Retrieve the service history of a Veteran using demographics to search for the Veteran.

description.content:
This endpoint provides a Veteran's detailed service history including all military
service episodes with the first and last name of the Veteran, the branch of service,
start and end dates, paygrade, discharge status, and separation reason. It also
provides start and end dates and locations for each deployment within each episode
of service.

Required information:
* First Name
* Last Name
* Date of Birth
* Street Address
* City
* State
* Country
* Zipcode

Optional information:
* Middle Name
* Gender

Tips for finding a match:
* First and Last Name, Address and DOB are often essential to uniquely identifying a
Veteran.
* Home Address is recommended, but it may be beneficial to retry the search with
additional addresses if they are available and the first attempt is not confirmed.
* To find a match, it can help to retry with different demographic information,
such as alternate last names or addresses.
* It can also help to remove special characters, hyphens, apostrophes, or spaces
in a name prior to matching.

NOTE: In the Sandbox only DOB and ZIP are required to identify the test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name: Service History retrieved successfully

item.name: Status
Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 Veteran.

request.name: Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 Veteran.

description.content:
Indicates whether an individual is "Confirmed" or "Not Confirmed" as a
 [Title 38 Veteran](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-38) according to VA.
  ```"not confirmed"``` can mean several different things, depending on
 the following not_confirmed_reason values:
- ```PERSON_NOT_FOUND```: The person was not found in VA source systems
 with the provided information. Including more demographic data
 for a Veteran increases the likelihood of finding their records
 in VA source systems.
 - ```NOT_TITLE_38```: VA data sources indicate that the person does not
 have Title 38 status.
 - ```MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED```: VA cannot confirm the person's Title 38
 status with existing records.
 - ```ERROR```: One or more VA source systems is not available or returned
 an error response.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name:
Confirmation status successfully retrieved.

Possible return values if Veteran is "NOT CONFIRMED":
 - ERROR
 - MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED
 - NOT_TITLE_38
 - PERSON_NOT_FOUND

item.name: Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 Veteran using demographics

request.name: Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 Veteran using demographics

description.content:
Confirm an individual's Title 38 Veteran status according to VA.
This endpoint accepts demographic information for the person to
be confirmed and matches it against VA records. If there are
differences between the data entered and the data on file, a
match to a person may not be found. If a successful match is
identified, Title 38 Veteran status is checked.

- ```"confirmed"``` indicates Title 38 Veteran status.
- ```"not confirmed"``` can mean several different things, depending on
the following not_confirmed_reason values:
    - ```PERSON_NOT_FOUND```: The person was not found in VA source systems
    with the provided information. Including more demographic data
    for a Veteran increases the likelihood of finding their records
    in VA source systems.
    - ```NOT_TITLE_38```: VA data sources indicate that the person does not
    have Title 38 status.
    - ```MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED```: VA cannot confirm the person's Title 38
    status with existing records.
    - ```ERROR```: One or more VA source systems is not available or returned
    an error response.

Required information:
* First Name
* Last Name
* Date of Birth
* Street Address
* City
* State
* Country
* Zipcode

Optional information:
* Middle Name
* Gender
* Mother's Maiden Name
* Home Phone Number
* Birth Place City
* Birth Place State
* Birth Place Country

Tips for finding a match:
* First and Last Name, Address and DOB are often essential to uniquely identifying a
Veteran.
* To find a match, it can help to retry with different demographic information, such
as alternate last names or addresses.
* It can also help to remove special characters, hyphens, apostrophes, or spaces in
a name prior to matching.
* If needed, Veterans may update their profile with the VA on VA.gov. The updated
information will be available to this API immediately. Additional field-level
guidance is provided in the schema section of this documentation.

NOTE: In the Sandbox only DOB and ZIP are required to identify the test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name:
Confirmation status successfully retrieved.

Possible return values if Veteran is "NOT CONFIRMED":
 - ERROR
 - MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED
 - NOT_TITLE_38
 - PERSON_NOT_FOUND

item.name: Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 veteran using Social Security number and demographics.

request.name: Confirm whether an individual is a Title 38 veteran using Social Security number and demographics.

description.content:
Confirm an individual's Title 38 Veteran status according to VA.
This endpoint accepts a Social Security number and/or demographic
information for the person to be confirmed and matches it against
VA records. If there are differences between the data entered and
the data on file, a match to a person may not be found. If a
successful match is identified, Title 38 Veteran status is checked.

- ```"confirmed"``` indicates Title 38 Veteran status.
- ```"not confirmed"``` can mean several different things, depending on
the following not_confirmed_reason values:
    - ```PERSON_NOT_FOUND```: The person was not found in VA source systems
    with the provided information. Including more demographic data
    for a Veteran increases the likelihood of finding their records
    in VA source systems.
    - ```NOT_TITLE_38```: VA data sources indicate that the person does not
    have Title 38 status.
    - ```MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED```: VA cannot confirm the person's Title 38
    status with existing records.
    - ```ERROR```: One or more VA source systems is not available or returned
    an error response.

If using Social Security number, the following information is required:
* Social Security number
* First name
* Last name
* Birth date

If using demographic data, the following information is required:
* First Name
* Last Name
* Birth date
* Street Address
* City
* State
* Country
* Zip code

The following optional information may also be used with Social Security number or
demographic data:
* Middle Name
* Gender
* Mother's Maiden Name
* Home Phone Number
* Birth Place City
* Birth Place State
* Birth Place Country

Tips for finding a match if using only demographic data:
* First and Last Name, Address and Birth date are often essential to uniquely identify a
Veteran.
* Home address is recommended, but it may also help to retry the search with
additional available addresses when the first attempt is not confirmed.
* To find a match, it can help to retry with different demographic information, such
as alternate last names or addresses.
* It may also help to remove special characters, hyphens, apostrophes, or spaces
in a name prior to matching.

NOTE: In the Sandbox only birth date and zip code are required to identify the
test user.

oauth2.value: aca_coverage_restricted.read disability_rating.read disability_rating_summary.read disability_rating_restricted.read disability_rating_summary_restricted.read enrolled_benefits.read flashes.read launch.read permanent_and_total_disability.read permanent_and_total_disability_restricted.read service_history.read status_restricted.read veteran_status.read

response.name:
Confirmation status successfully retrieved.

Possible return values if Veteran is "NOT CONFIRMED":
 - ERROR
 - MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED
 - NOT_TITLE_38
 - PERSON_NOT_FOUND

info.name: Veteran Service History and Eligibility API

description.content:
With this API you can
* Return enrolled benefits for a Veteran.
* Return a Veteran's enrollment status in minimum essential coverage (MEC) as defined by
the Affordable Care Act (ACA).  (restricted access).
* Return disability ratings of a Veteran.
* Return certain benefit flashes for a Veteran.
* Return the service history of a Veteran.
* Confirm an individual's Title 38 Veteran status.

This information is more detailed than the information returned by the [The Veteran Confirmation API](https://developer.va.gov/explore/verification/docs/veteran_confirmation?version=current) and as such, is only available to authorized and authenticated consumers.

# Technical overview

You can use the POST or GET version of each endpoint regardless of your
authorization method.

## Using GET endpoints

The GET endpoint relies on the ICN to find a specific matching Veteran. When
using OAuth, the ICN is not required as part of your request. The ICN is
automatically added as part of the OAuth launch token context.

When using CCG, you must add the ICN in the launch context for the OAuth token.

## Using POST endpoints

POST endpoints use identifiable information to find a match instead of a
Veteran's ICN. The required information is listed in the parameter
descriptions for these endpoints. Successful responses are received and
Title 38 status is checked when a single Veteran's data matches with
confidence to the submitted data.

## Not confirmed reasons
A result of "not confirmed" does not necessarily mean that a person is not a Veteran.
A person may have served without being entitled to the VA benefits of a Title 38 Veteran,
or more research may be required to determine their status. If one of the /status endpoints
returns a veteran_status of "not confirmed", it will also return one of the following
not_confirmed_reason values:

  - ```PERSON_NOT_FOUND``` : The person was not found in VA source systems with
  the provided information.
      - **Suggested action**: Re-check information and try query again. Including
      more demographic data for a Veteran increases the likelihood of finding
      their records in VA source systems.
  - ```NOT_TITLE_38```: VA data sources indicate that the person does not have
  Title 38 status.
      - **Suggested action**: The person should contact VA if they believe they
          should have Title 38 status.
  - ```MORE_RESEARCH_REQUIRED```: VA cannot confirm the person's Title 38 status
      with existing records.
      - **Suggested action**: The Veteran should request a manual review from VA
          if they believe that they should have Title 38 status.
  - ```ERROR```: One or more VA source systems is not available or returned
      an error response.
      - **Suggested action**:  Run the request again.

## Authentication and Authorization

The Veteran Service History and Eligibility API uses OAuth 2.0/OpenID
Connect. The following authorization models are supported:
* [Authorization code flow](https://developer.va.gov/explore/authorization/docs/authorization-code?api=veteran_verification)
* [Client credentials grant](https://developer.va.gov/explore/authorization/docs/client-credentials?api=veteran_verification)

**Important:**
- To get production access using client credentials grant, you must either work for VA or
 have specific VA agreements in place.
-  Some endpoints have additional access restrictions. To get sandbox or production access
 to individual endpoints labeled (restricted access),
 you must be an approved Federal consumer of the data they provide.
- If you have questions about these API access restrictions, [contact us](https://developer.va.gov/support/contact-us).

## Test the API using Sandbox

We use [mock test data](https://developer.va.gov/explore/api/veteran-service-history-and-eligibility/test-users) in the sandbox environment. APIs accessed via the Sandbox environment are using the same underlying logic as VA's production APIs; only the underlying data store is different.

Use these test user attributes in sandbox to populate the request body as only
test users can return a "CONFIRMED" response. For sandbox POST requests, only a
test user's date of birth and zip code are used to identify a Veteran.


Contact Support:
 Name: VA.gov
